data.title: Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer is latest Trump official to resign
data.description:
Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is resigning from her post amid an internal investigation into allegations of misconduct, making her the third woman to exit the Trump administration in recent weeks.

Chavez-DeRemer "will be leaving the administration to take a position in the private sector," Steven Cheung, White House communications director, announced. He added that Keith Sonderling, deputy labor secretary, will take on the role of acting secretary.

The Labor Department's inspector general was investigating complaints of Chavez-DeRemer having an affair with a subordinate, drinking on the job, and visiting friends and family on the taxpayers' dime, the New York Post reported in January.

Her husband, Shawn DeRemer, was barred from the Labor Department's headquarters because of sexual assault allegations, The New York Times reported in February. Federal prosecutors closed the investigation into the matter without bringing charges.

Chavez-DeRemer's resignation follows two other high-profile exits from the Trump administration – Kristi Noem, who previously led the Department of Homeland Security, and Pam Bondi, former attorney general.

data.title: Forestar Group GAAP EPS of $0.63 in-line, revenue of $374.3M beats by $0.65M
data.description:
• Forestar Group press release (FOR): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.63 in-line.
  • Revenue of $374.3M (+6.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.65M.
  • Owned and controlled 94,400 lots
  • 24,100 lots contracted for sale representing $2.2 billion of future revenue
  • Real estate of $2.7 billion
  • Total liquidity of $1.0 billion
  • Net debt to total capital ratio of 19.2%
  • Return on equity of 9.6% for the trailing twelve months ended March 31, 2026
  • Book value per share increased 10% to $35.66

data.title: Peoples Bancorp to buy Citizens National in $76.6M deal
data.description:
Peoples Bancorp (PEBO) said it will acquire Citizens National (CZNL), the parent company of Citizens Bank of Kentucky, in a cash-and-stock deal valued at about $76.6M.

The deal values Citizens at $78.39 per share based on Peoples' 20-day average price. It is expected to close during the second half of 2026.

Under the terms of the merger agreement, Citizens will merge with and into Peoples, and Citizens Bank will subsequently merge with and into Peoples' wholly owned subsidiary, Peoples Bank. Shareholders of Citizens will receive 2.10 shares of Peoples common stock plus $8.00 in cash for each share of Citizens' common stock.

Citizens, through its community bank subsidiary and 132 employees, operates 12 branches located primarily in Eastern Kentucky. As of March 31, 2026, Citizens had, on a consolidated basis, $686M in total assets, which included $342M in gross loans, and $586M in total deposits.

The transaction is expected to be immediately accretive to Peoples' estimated earnings, with a tangible book value earnback of less than one year and an internal rate of return in excess of 20%.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Danaher tops Q1 earnings targets, lifts FY26 guidance despite slight revenue miss
France sees €4B-€6B impact from Iran crisis amid rising yields, finance minister says
data.description:
France Finance Minister Roland Lescure said that France has incurred an economic cost of €4B to €6B due to the ongoing Iran crisis driven largely by surging energy prices and rising borrowing costs.

Bond yields have surged since the onset of the Middle East conflict, with the resulting rise in government borrowing costs alone adding roughly €3.6B to the budget, Lescure told France's RTL radio.

Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu's administration pledged to fully offset the fiscal impact of measures introduced to shield households from the energy price shock. Lescure added that he will present plans to freeze certain areas of public spending during a meeting with lawmakers on Tuesday.

France, which already runs one of the largest budget deficits in the eurozone, can afford support measures that are strictly targeted at those most in need. Far-right parties have called for a reduction in the 20% value-added tax on fuel, while left-wing groups are pushing for broader interventions, including caps on energy prices.

The Iran war has pushed up inflation due to a surge in energy prices and strained energy-intensive sectors like transport, agriculture, and fishing.

data.title: Synchrony Financial declares $0.30 dividend, $0.34 from next quarter, $6.5B buy back program
data.description:
• Synchrony Financial (SYF) declares $0.30/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.53%
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record May 5; ex-div May 5.
  • Additionally, the Company plans to increase the quarterly cash dividend from $0.30 to $0.34 per share of common stock beginning in third quarter 2026.
  • The Company also approved a new common share repurchase program of up to $6.5 billion, replacing prior program, which was scheduled to expire on June 30, 2026 and had approximately $300 million remaining.
  • The program commences in the second quarter of 2026 and does not have an expiration date. 
  • The new share repurchase program replaces the Company's  
  • See SYF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Peoples Bancorp GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.01, revenue of $119.3M beats by $0.58M
data.description:
• Peoples Bancorp press release (PEBO): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $119.3M (+5.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.58M.
  • Net interest margin for the first quarter of 2026 increased to 4.16% when compared to 4.12% for the linked quarter driven by a reduction in deposit costs.
  • The provision for credit losses was $9.7 million for the first quarter of 2026, compared to $8.1 million for the linked quarter and $10.2 million for the first quarter of 2025.
